article_title: Two Simultaneous Papers Report Field Re-Entrant Superconductivity in Europium-Doped Nickelates, Defying the Pauli Limit

content_preview.title: Two Simultaneous Papers Report Field Re-Entrant Superconductivity in Europium-Doped Nickelates, Defying the Pauli Limit
content_preview.summary: Independent teams publishing in Nature and Nature Communications show that doping infinite-layer nickelates with europium revives superconductivity at magnetic fields that should destroy it.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Two independent research groups published convergent findings in late April 2026 showing that europium substitution in infinite-layer nickelates can revive superconductivity at magnetic field strengths that ordinarily destroy electron pairing. A Nature paper published online on [23 April 2026 by Yang, Tang, Wu and colleagues](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-026-10547-y) and a Nature Communications paper from a Yale-led collaboration that [Yale Engineering announced](https://e...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Substantial physics piece (873 words) with strong technical sourcing across 6 sources. All scientific specifics verified.

source_verification.https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-0: source-0.html — Nature paper 'Field re-entrant superconductivity in Eu-doped infinite-layer nickelates', datePublished 2026-04-23. Title and metadata verified.
source_verification.https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-0: source-1.html — Nature Communications paper 'Re-entrant unconventional superconductivity induced by rare-earth substitution in Nd1-xEuxNiO2 thin films', datePublished 2026-03-05. Title verified.
source_verification.https://engineering.yale.edu/news-and-ev: source-2.html — Yale Engineering announcement dated April 22, 2026. Quotes verified verbatim: 'Reaching that point took us months to years of process optimization, because nickelate films are highly unstable' and 'europium ions appear to partially shield the superconducting electron pairs from the applied field, helping the superconducting state survive to much higher magnetic fields'.
source_verification.https://phys.org/news/2026-04-fragile-ni: source-3.html — Phys.org summary, datePublished 2026-04-27. 'World's strongest magnets' Vu quote present (also in Yale Engineering source-2).
source_verification.https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.15968: source-4.html — Yale paper preprint. Verified: Dung Vu lead + 19 collaborators (20 authors total), Charles Ahn as PI, Eu-doping x = 0.2 to 0.35, 2Δ/k_BTc ≈ 5–6 verbatim, 'strongly violates the weak-coupling Pauli limit' verbatim.
source_verification.https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.14666: source-5.html — Nature paper preprint. Verified: Yang, Tang, Wu plus 19 additional authors (22 total), Eu-doped Sm-Ca nickelate system, exchange-field compensation mechanism.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All scientific claims verified: author counts on both papers, Pauli-limit violation, gap-to-Tc ratio 5-6, Eu doping concentrations, Nd1-xEuxNiO2 thin films, Sm-Ca nickelate host, direct quotes from Vu, the 'partially shield' mechanism explanation. Internal cross-references to prior Penn State iron-telluride and Rice magneto-ARPES articles are appropriate.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and technical. The 'What We Don't Know' section honestly flags Tc uncertainty, host-material specificity, and bulk reproducibility.
editor_notes.originality: Eu-doped nickelate field-re-entrant superconductivity not previously covered. Internal cross-references appropriate.
concerns: Chronology framing: Overview opens 'Two independent research groups published convergent findings in late April 2026'. The Nature paper (Yang/Tang/Wu) was published April 23, 2026, but the Nature Communications paper (Yale) was actually published March 5, 2026. What converged in late April was the public visibility (Yale Engineering announcement April 22, Nature paper April 23, Phys.org April 27). The article body never explicitly states the Yale paper's publication date, so body framing is technically defensible. Headline 'Two Simultaneous Papers' is similarly loose. Non-blocking but worth flagging.
recommendations: Optional clean-up: rephrase the Overview opening to clarify that what's late-April is the convergent public visibility (announcements + Nature publication), not both papers' actual publication dates.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Solid physics reporting with all scientific specifics verified across 6 sources including both arXiv preprints. One minor framing issue about the 'simultaneous' chronology is worth flagging but doesn't rise to a blocker — the convergence of two independent groups on Eu-doped nickelate field re-entrant superconductivity is real and properly sourced.
